text:
Day 13 of theOlympicsis already well underway withTeam GBwinning their 13th gold in Paris courtesy of Eleanor Aldridge in the kite surfing.

Action started early this morning with the women’s 10km marathon swim in the Seine with Leah Crisp competing for Team GB, but it was Netherlands’ Sharon Van Rouwendaal who took gold.

There were high hopes for Jade Jones to become the first three-time Olympic taekwondo champion, but she lost her first fight in devastating fashion.

Bradly Sinden won silver in Tokyo but the British taekwondo star was cruelly forced to pull out of his bronze medal match due to an injury sustained in his semi-final bout.

On the track, Noah Lyles was looking todouble up on gold after his 100m success, but the American was forced to settle for bronze beforerevealing he had tested positive for Covid.

There was plenty of optimism from a British perspective to end the day, though, withKatarina Johnson-Thompsonin gold medal position heading into the second day of the women’s heptathlon.

This blog is now closed. For the latest updates, visitMetro’s Sport page.

Another topsy-turvy day of Olympic action draws to a close and it included a welcome gold for Team GB – their first in three days.

Can they add to their tally tomorrow? Only time will tell…

Regardless, we’ll be back then to guide you through it all – goodnight!

Plenty to come tomorrow – here’s the crucial timings for those looking to catch it all.

Athletics

Basketball

Beach volleyball

Boxing

Canoe sprint

Cycling (track)

Diving

Football

Golf

Handball

Hockey

Modern pentathlon

Rhythmic gymnastics

Sport climbing

Swimming

Table tennis

Taekwondo

Volleyball

Water polo

Weightlifting

Wrestling

Here’s how things look after a busy Day 13 in Paris. USA open up a slight advantage over China at the top of the table, but it’s still very tight between GB and France for fourth place.

Hasnboy Dusmatov of Uzebkistan lets out a roar as he’s declared unanimous winner and gold medal winner in the men’s 51kg final.

It’s silver for France’s Billal Bennama.

The United States are heavy favourites to claim gold in the men’s basketball but had to ovefrcame a huge scare against Serbia to reach the final tonight.

Trailing 63-76 after the third quarter, it was an inspired display from LeBron James and Steph Curry to guide their nation to a narrow 95-91 win.

They’ll face France in the gold-medal match.

The United States are through to the gold medal game ðºð¸It’s heartbreak for Serbia after a huge comeback from the four-time defending Olympic champions.#BBCOlympics#Paris2024#Olympicspic.twitter.com/0GTVvIrqrx

BBChost Gabby Logan has suggested Noah Lyles made a ‘selfish decision’ to compete in the men’s 200m final at theParisOlympics after contracting Covid-19.

Read all she had to say below:

Arshad Nadeem of Pakistan sets a huge new world record of 92.97 metres to beat defending champion Neeraj Chopra to gold.

An OLYMPIC RECORD in the men’s javelin final from Pakistan’s Arshad Nadeem! ð®ð#BBCOlympics#Olympics#Paris2024pic.twitter.com/9CLDHLetJT

It’s a slender 48-point lead for Johnson-Thompson over reigning Olympic champion Nafi Thiam heading into the second and final day.

It’s fair to say the Team GB athlete is pleased with her efforts:

‘It’s probably the best thing I’ve ever done in my career’ ðªKatarina Johnson-Thompson LOVED her shot put personal best on day 1 of the women’s heptathlon, and dedicated her celebration to@RealDeniseLewisin the studio â¤ï¸#BBCOlympics#Olympics#Paris2024pic.twitter.com/oJjKfyL9w4

The US will surely end the day atop the medal table after a flurry of late golds inside the Stade de France.

Tara Davis-Woodhall claims long jump gold moments before Grant Holloway romps home in first to take the 110m hurdles crown.

A storming run from the American in the women’s 400m hurdles final as McLaughlin-Levrone defends her gold medal, setting a new world record in the process.

Arch-rival Femke Bol faded towards the end and has to settle for bronze.

Lyles has been speaking to US broadcaster NBC and said he may be forced to skip Friday’s relay final due to his positive Covid test.

‘At the minute I don’t know but I’m feeling more on the side of letting Team USA do their own thing,’ he said.

‘They’ve proven with great certainty that they can handle it without me and if that is the case coming off today then I’m perfectly fine to let them go do their thing. They have more than enough speed to handle it and get the gold medal.’

The 200m is one of Johnson-Thompson’s strongest events but she looks a tad disappointed despite crossing the line first in a time of 23.45 seconds.

Still, that’s good enough to move her back above Nafi Thiam who finished her heat second with a time of 24.46 seconds.

A new development from the men’s 200m as it’s revealed that Noah Lyles was racing after a positive Covid-19 test.

Details here:

‘Tebogo… and Letsile Tebogo… AND LETSILE TEBOGO COMING THROUGH!’

Sit back and enjoy Letsile Tebogo’s sensational performance in the men’s 200m final.

Nobody was getting between him and the gold medal this evening…

19.45 ðð®Botswana’s Letsile Tebogo is the men’s 200m Olympic champion! He wins his nation’s first EVER Olympic gold. ðNoah Lyles has to settle for the bronze medal.#BBCOlympics#Olympics#Paris2024pic.twitter.com/3sFKcMgF1R

Letsile Tebogo of Botswana is the new men’s 200m Olympic champion!

Just three months after the death of his mother, Tebogo claims gold with a sensational time of 19.46 seconds at a rapturous Stade de France. It’s Botswana’s first ever Olympic gold medal.

The 100m final was all about Noah Lyles, but the American must settle for bronze this time around.

Lyles’ fellow countryman Kenny Bednarek takes the silver.

Katarina Johnson-Thompson has thrown a personal best in the shot put âï¸This heptathlon competition ð¥#BBCOlympics#Paris2024#Olympicspic.twitter.com/TsdiO1PF7M

Katarina Johnson-Thompson has put herself in a commanding position on the first day of the women’s heptathlon.

The Team GB athlete, a two-time world champion, sits second after an enormous personal-best throw of 14.44m in the shot put this evening.

It comes after Johnson-Thompson recorded a season’s best time of 13.40 seconds in the 100m hurdles.

Olympic champion Nafi Thiam, the double Olympic champion from Belgium, leads on 3070 points as it stands.

Bradly Sinden has been forced to withdraw from the the men’s taekwondo bronze-medal match due to injury.

The Team GB fighter, a silver medallist in Tokyo, appeared to be struggling with a knee problem towards the end of his semi-final with Zaid Abdul Kareem previously.

‘In my second fight against Croatia, I felt a twinge in my left knee, my MCL, and then carried on in the fight and felt it go,’ Sinden told BBC Sport this evening.

‘With the injury, I just didn’t think I had it in me to perform in the bronze-medal match and bring home a medal. I didn’t want to end Paris 2024 like this but sometimes your body has different plans for you.’

France’s Benjamin Thomas takes the host nation’s gold-medal haul to 14!

Despite crashing, Thomas keeps his composure and takes the roof off the Saint-Quentin-en-Yvelines Velodrome with a roaring victory in the men’s omnium.

Portugal’s Iuri Leitao and Belgium’s Fabio Van den Bossche take silver and bronze respectively, while Ethan Hayter of Great Britain finishes down in eighth place.

Carmarthen to the world! ð´ó §ó ¢ó ·ó ¬ó ³ó ¿Keirin bronze medallist Emma Finucane is delighted to add to her tally at this games!#BBCOlympics#Olympics#Paris2024pic.twitter.com/eEdBZkuKku

Emma Finucane is on top of the world after winning a second medal at her debut Olympic Games with Team GB.

‘To get a bronze medal, it literally feels like gold to me because I left everything out there on the track,’ the 21-year-old told BBC Sport after her third-placed finish in the women’s keirin.

‘If you told me a year ago I’d be coming to the Olympics and getting a gold in track sprint and then bronze in keirin, I would’ve been like, “No way!”, but here I am.’

What drama!

After being handed a reprieve, Team GB’s Jack Carlin progresses to the semi-finals of the men’s sprint with victory in his deciding third race against Japan’s Kaiya Ota.

Ota had appeared to have secured his spot in the next round until his misdemeanour in the second race was penalised – and the Japanese competitor it now OUT.

But will the officials get involved again? A stewards’ inquiry may be in the works after the pair once again made contact.



ArrowMORE:Gabby Logan suggests Noah Lyles made ‘selfish decision’ running 200m Olympic final with Covid

ArrowMORE:American sprinter accuses Olympic organisers of ‘favouritism’ ahead of 200m final

ArrowMORE:Another triathlete falls ill after swimming in filthy River Seine at Paris Olympics

title: Olympics schedule and results: Katarina Johnson-Thompson in heptathlon action | Metro News
description: Latest news and live updates from 2024 Paris Olympic Games on day 13, with athletics, cycling and boxing all on the schedule.
